items.title: China says banned 46 US entities from public procurement markets
items.summary: BEIJING: China said Monday it had banned public agencies from buying products from 46 US entities, including Lockheed Martin, Raytheon and Boeing's defence division. Companies with US investments in China are excluded, according to a statement from China's finance ministry, which said measures would take effect from Monday. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Titled land seized': Gua Musang settlers cry foul as farmland declared forest reserve
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: More than 100 settlers at the Kesedar Chalil Land Development Scheme here are in limbo after learning that the land they have cultivated for nearly 20 years has been classified as a forest reserve and seized. The affected residents, some of whom already hold land titles, claimed they were previously led to understand the plot was state land earmarked for cultivation and legitimate ownership. "Yet, when we conducted checks with the Gua Musang District and Land Office back in 2008, it was classified as state land, and we were told titles could be issued upon paying the land premium," Hasan said.

items.title: Iran FM Araghchi signals 'major breakthrough' in US talks
items.summary: BURGENSTOCK, Switzerland: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Monday there was "major progress" in talks with the United States. "Pakistani and Qatari mediation has delivered major progress to end Lebanon War," Araghchi posted on X. "Oil and petrochem exports are waived, blockade lifted, some frozen assets released, and major reconstruction & development plan launched for Iran."

items.title: Muda unveils trio of fresh Johor candidates as Amira Aisya builds youthful election slate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) today announced three more candidates who will contest in the Johor state election. Muda Kulai chief M. Premanand, 53, will contest the Bukit Batu state seat, while Muda Muar information chief Muhammad Amir Fiqri, 30, has been named for Maharani, and the party’s secretary-general Ainie Haziqah Shafii, 36, will contest Simpang Jeram. Yesterday, Amira Aisya announced Puteri Wangsa State Constituency Service Centre chief Rashifa Aljunied, 26, as the party’s candidate for the Puteri Wangsa seat.

items.title: Online threats, insults fuel South Africa's anti-foreigner hate
items.summary: INFLAMMATORY social media posts – including of men brandishing a machete or calling foreigners "leeches" – are stoking emotions around an unofficial demand for illegal immigrants to leave South Africa by the end of the month, fuelling a volatile situation, analysts say. "You know the impact of social media these days: when people say they're going to kill you, you can't sleep," he told AFP. Social media has also been effective in rallying South Africans behind radical citizen-led groups mobilising against undocumented migrants, experts said.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia opens lower on cautious tone
items.summary: Among heavyweights, PPB Group was down by 44 sen to RM9.68, IOI Corporation fell six sen to RM4.27, Petronas Dagangan eased 18 sen to RM18.56, Maxis slid three sen to RM3.39, and Maybank was eight sen lower at RM11.10. Of the most active stocks, Dagang Nexchange added half-a-sen to 47 sen, AirAsia X was five sen weaker at RM1.28, Widad Group and Tanco were flat each at two sen and 14 sen, respectively, and Capital A declined 1.5 sen to 42.5 sen. Among top gainers, F&N rose 30 sen to RM28.38, Concrete Engineering Products (Cepco) gained 26 sen to RM3.97, Skyechip increased 11 sen to RM3.20, Petronas Chemicals Group ticked up nine sen to RM,4.58, and Hong Leong Bank was eight sen firmer at RM22.24.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei rises past 72,000 mark for first time as AI euphoria persists
items.summary: The benchmark Nikkei 225 advanced 1.4 per cent to 72,247.21 in early trading after touching a record intraday high of 72,269.64. The broader Topix gained 1.1 per cent to 4,089.59. Nonferrous metals led Topix sector gains, up 7.57 per cent, followed by electric appliances and glass & ceramics products, which rose 2.08 per cent and 2.05 per cent respectively.

items.title: RHB Research keeps 'Buy' call on VS Industry, lowers target price on weaker earnings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: VS Industry Bhd is expected to stage a gradual earnings recovery from the fourth quarter of financial year 2026 (Q4 FY26) onwards, according to RHB Research. "VS Industry's healthy balance sheet positions it well to capture market share amidst a period of industry consolidation," it said. Meanwhile, the firm also said VS Insustry's nine-month FY26 results came in below expectations, as the group posted a core net loss of RM31.9 million.

items.title: Gold rebounds from one-week low as Iran cites progress in peace talks
items.summary: BENGALURU: Gold rose over one per cent on Monday, rebounding from a more than one-week low hit in the previous session, as Brent crude oil prices fell after Iran claimed progress in US-Iran peace talks, easing concerns around inflation and higher interest rates. US gold futures for August delivery fell 0.5 per cent to US$4,225.80. Brent crude futures fell 0.5 per cent after Iran claimed progress in peace talks, easing fears of elevated inflation and higher-for-longer global interest rates.

items.title: Identity card and motorcycle help police identify missing man found dead in Baling, missing since June 10
items.summary: BALING, June 22 — The body of a 58-year-old man, who had been reported missing for 12 days, was found in a rubber plantation near the Felda Teloi Kanan intersection here yesterday. Baling district police chief Supt Brandon Anak Richard Joe said police received a call at 11.50am informing them of the discovery of a body at the location. “A black Modenas motorcycle was also found near the victim,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Supply restocking may support crude above pre-conflict levels: CIMB Securities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Inventory rebuilding by countries following recent supply disruptions could underpin global oil demand and keep crude prices above pre-conflict levels, according to CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd. The firm said Brent crude prices of US$75 to US$80 per barrel suggest the market is pricing in a relatively smooth normalisation of oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz. On the demand front, CIMB Securities said weaker-than-expected oil consumption in China could weigh on prices.

items.title: Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman lands in Malaysia for first official visit since taking office
items.summary: SEPANG, June 22 — Bangladesh Prime Minister Tarique Rahman arrived in Malaysia yesterday for an official visit to further strengthen the close bilateral relations between the two countries and deepen cooperation in areas of mutual interest. The visit at the invitation of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, marks Tarique’s first bilateral official visit abroad since assuming office in February 2026. Welcoming Tarique at BRC was Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Datuk Zulkifli Hasan.

items.title: Iran-US agree on 60-day roadmap to end war
items.summary: BURGENSTOCK, Switzerland: Iran and the United States agreed on a roadmap towards reaching a final deal to end the war within 60 days, mediators Pakistan and Qatar said in a joint statement on Monday. "The High Level Committee has agreed upon a roadmap towards reaching a final deal within 60 days, laying the foundation for the immediate commencement of further technical talks," the joint statement read.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Johor state election:PM Anwar to announce list of PH candidates tonight
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to announce the full list of 56 candidates for the coalition for the 16th Johor State Election. PH's top leadership, including DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke and Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu, are among those scheduled to be present tonight, in addition to the coalition's machinery and supporters. So far, PH through DAP has announced five candidates, namely lawyer Chu Poh Yee (Mengkibol), private secretary to the Deputy Finance Minister Nor Zulaila Abd Ghani (Tiram), Johor DAP Socialist Youth (DAPSY) chief Lee Wern Yiing (Johor Jaya), former incumbent Wong Bor Yang (Senai) and special assistant to Kulai Member of Parliament Mohamad Shafwan Ani (Bukit) Permai.

items.title: Police arrest five suspects after disappearance of teenage cousins in Kota Bharu sparks urgent rescue
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 22 — Police have detained five local men, aged between their teens and 20s, for allegedly being involved in the disappearance of two teenage girls, aged 15 and 16, who were rescued in an operation in Machang yesterday. Machang district police chief Supt Azuhar Md Nor said all five suspects were arrested in Machang Saturday afternoon. “Police had obtained remand orders against the suspects from the Machang Magistrate’s Court, with two key suspects remanded for five days and the remaining three for two days to facilitate investigations,” he said when contacted yesterday.

items.title: Dollar firms as cracks emerge in peace deal, pound dips on Starmer uncertainty
items.summary: That lifted oil prices, with Brent crude futures climbing 1.30 per cent to US$81.62 a barrel. The pound was 0.24 per cent weaker at US$1.32055, while the euro softened 0.1 per cent to US$1.1462. The Australian dollar was last down 0.19 per cent at US$0.70035, while the New Zealand dollar last bought US$0.573.

items.title: PM Anwar hails Sarawak’s expertise as Bintulu Port shifts from federal to state control
items.summary: KUCHING, June 22 — The Federal Government is confident that the Sarawak State Government has the expertise and capability to operate Bintulu Port, which has been changed from a federal port to a state port. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the port is an important port, especially for the country’s liquefied natural gas (LNG) industry and the Asian region. The Cheque Handover Ceremony for the Change of Status of Bintulu Port from a federal port to a state port was held in Bintulu, today.

items.title: Stocks slip in Asia, oil up on peace doubts
items.summary: Iran's threats were enough to push Brent crude futures up 1.1 per cent to US$81.43 a barrel, still far below its May peak of US$126.41. S&P 500 futures eased 0.5 per cent, while Nasdaq futures lost 0.7 per cent. In Europe, EUROSTOXX 50 futures fell 0.5 per cent, while DAX futures dropped 0.3 per cent and FTSE futures dipped 0.1 per cent.

items.title: #NSTviral: Netizens stunned by woman changing bulb atop Petronas Twin Towers [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A viral video showing maintenance workers carrying out repairs near the pinnacle of one of the Petronas Twin Towers has left social media users both amazed and terrified by the dizzying heights involved. Posted on TikTok by user Siva wwc, the footage shows a team of workers secured with safety harnesses as they perform maintenance work on the uppermost section of one of the iconic towers. one user asked.

items.title: Hizbollah rejects Israeli 'security zone' in southern Lebanon
items.summary: BEIRUT: Hizbollah chief Naim Qassem on Sunday rejected any Israeli security zone in Lebanon, after Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed his troops would remain in the country's south for "as long as necessary." Israeli troops "remaining on Lebanese land is impossible. Israel's defence minister meanwhile said that Israeli forces had standing orders to act against any threat they encountered inside Lebanon.

items.title: Oil rises after Iran shuts Hormuz again, Trump threatens new attacks
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Oil prices rose on Monday after shipping through the Strait of Hormuz slowed while talks between US and Iranian officials in their first meeting under an interim peace deal got off to a bumpy start. US West Texas Intermediate crude futures were at US$78.62 a barrel, up US$2.02, or 2.64 per cent, ahead of the contract's expiry later on Monday. The more active August contract rose US$1.43 to US$77.28 a barrel.

items.title: Israel kills two near West Bank settlement
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israel's military said Sunday that they had killed two people who threw Molotov cocktails near the West Bank settlement of Karmei Tzur. Soldiers moved against a group of people burning tyres and throwing petrol bombs towards the settlement, the military said in a statement. Israel has occupied the West Bank since 1967.

items.title: In Dewan Rakyat today: Parliament kicks off with Hormuz crisis queries, Haj reforms and AI safeguard
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The economic impact of trade disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz and measures to improve the haj management system next year are among the key issues to be discussed on the opening day of the Second Meeting of the Fifth Session of the 15th Parliament today. According to the Order Paper on Parliament’s official website, Datuk Dr Richard Rapu @ Aman anak Begri (GPS-Betong) is scheduled to ask the Economy Minister about the latest assessment of the impact of trade disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz on local industries, especially operating costs, and the national inflation rate for the second quarter of 2026 during the questions for oral answers session. Wong Shu Qi (PH-Kluang) will ask the Digital Minister whether the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Governance Bill currently being drafted will explicitly address the misuse of AI, including the creation of deepfake child sexual exploitation material, identity spoofing and the dissemination of explicit content without consent.

items.title: Lime plans to name Uber as anchor investor in IPO
items.summary: NEW YORK: Lime, which operates an electric bike and scooter network, plans to name ride-hailing firm Uber as an anchor investor in its US initial public offering, The Information reported on Sunday, citing a source. Uber and Lime did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment. San Francisco-based startup Lime is backed by Uber Technologies.

items.title: Japan finance minister vows readiness to act on yen at any time
items.summary: TOKYO: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said on Monday that authorities are prepared to respond appropriately to currency moves at any time, repeating the warning as the yen trades near a four-decade low. "I would refrain from commenting on current foreign exchange situations, but we will respond appropriately to foreign exchange as needed at any time," Katayama said at a regular press conference. A break above the currency pair's 2024 high of 161.96 would send the yen to its weakest level since 1986.

items.title: Thunderstorm warning issued for Putrajaya, Labuan and 10 states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Meteorological Department (METMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm, strong winds and heavy rain warning for the Federal Territories of Putrajaya and Labuan, as well as 10 other states, effective until 9am today. In a statement, METMalaysia said the affected states include Perlis, Penang and Kedah, specifically Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Kulim and Bandar Baharu. Apart from Putrajaya and Labuan, areas in Perak (Kerian, Larut, Matang dan Selama, Manjung, Perak Tengah, Bagan Datuk and Hilir Perak) and Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Klang, Petaling, Kuala Langat and Sepang) are also expected to experience heavy rain and strong winds.

items.title: Ringgit eases versus greenback as traders monitor Middle East tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened lower against the US dollar on Monday, but strengthened against other major currencies amid cautious trading as market players closely watched Middle East geopolitical tensions unfolding. At 8am, the local note slipped to 4.1350/1445 against the greenback from last Friday's close of 4.1340/1395. Mohd Afzanizam said the ringgit would trade sideways against the greenback amid weak market sentiment.

items.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in DR Congo cross 1,000 as new infections recorded
items.summary: KIGALI (Rwanda): The Democratic Republic of Congo's total confirmed Ebola virus cases surged beyond 1,000 Sunday as new infections were recorded, said health authorities. Confirmed cases of the disease have climbed to 1,003, including 254 deaths, since the outbreak was declared on May 15, with a mortality rate of 25.3 per cent, Anadolu Ajansi reported Health Ministry update showed. "Despite this progression, response teams continue active investigations, epidemiological surveillance and prevention actions in affected areas," the ministry said.

items.title: Trump vows immediate fix as algae, peeling paint plague Reflecting Pool project
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said on Sunday work would begin "immediately" on fixing his beleaguered renovation project of the Reflecting Pool in Washington, which has been blighted with algae and peeling paint. Trump has championed a makeover for the 610-meter-long pool, bringing in contractors to drain it and paint it "American flag blue." "Work will begin immediately on fixing the seriously vandalised Reflecting Pool," Trump wrote Sunday on his Truth Social platform.

items.title: Australian police seize record 2.7 tonnes of cocaine
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australian police seized a record 2.7 tonnes of cocaine hidden in plastic tubs buried underground on the outskirts of Sydney, detectives said Monday. It was the largest cocaine haul in Australian history, a joint organised crime investigation force said in a statement. Police discovered the cocaine on Friday in plastic tubs buried in underground bunkers that were concealed by false floors, police said.

items.title: Why Uncertainty Has Become the New Currency of Power
items.summary: Yet the lessons from the 2026 Hormuz crisis extend far beyond energy markets. Even as diplomatic efforts gradually reduced tensions and shipping traffic resumed, commercial confidence remained fragile. War-risk insurance premiums stayed elevated, shipping schedules remained disrupted, and businesses continued to prepare for further uncertainty.

items.title: Insurance premiums may remain high despite Hormuz reopening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: War-risk insurance premiums are expected to stay high even after the Strait of Hormuz reopens, according to maritime analyst and scholar Nazery Khalid. The rebound in Gulf shipping could be slow as insurers and shipowners remain wary over whether the US-Iran peace deal will hold following June 15 truce. Marine underwriters are likely to stay cautious, viewing the agreement as a fragile truce amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainty, Nazery added.

items.title: Tabung Haji sharpens shift to customer-centric financial institution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lembaga Tabung Haji is stepping up efforts to transform itself from being perceived largely as a haj management and travel facilitator into a customer-centric Islamic savings and financial institution. Tabung Haji chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rashid Hussain said the shift forms part of a broader strategy to enhance its long-term sustainability, strengthen its financial position and foster deeper relationships with depositors. "Our ambition is for Malaysians to treat Tabung Haji as their premier savings institution.

items.title: Mobility more important than new highways, says ALR CEO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Amanat Lebuhraya Rakyat Bhd (ALR) group chief executive officer Muhammad Nizam Alias believes the future of Malaysian travel lies not merely in building highways, but in rethinking mobility itself. "We foresee that in the future, mobility is more important than building highways. Nizam, who was formerly Prasarana Malaysia Bhd president and group chief executive officer, said mobility could extend beyond highways, involving greater integration with public transit.

items.title: Nanta: Multi-lane fast flow rollout must not hit motorists' pockets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will not interfere in talks between highway concessionaires over the implementation of multi-lane fast flow (MLFF) tolling system but it comes with one firm condition: Road users must not be made to pay more. Nanta said the goal was a single, seamless system that road users could navigate without confusion, regardless of which concessionaire operated the stretch of highway they travel on. "They must have one complete system, fully integrated, so that road users can use it seamlessly.

items.title: Trump-backed candidate 'The Tiger' wins razor-tight Colombia presidential vote
items.summary: BOGOTÁ: A flamboyant US-backed lawyer who has never held public office narrowly won Colombia's presidential runoff Sunday, swinging the country hard to the right on a promise to wage war against drug-running guerrilla groups. With more than 99 per cent of polling centers reporting, Abelardo de la Espriella had 49.65 per cent of the vote, an unassailable lead over his rival, left-wing Senator Ivan Cepeda who trailed at 48.70 percent, according to official results. In Bogota, De la Espriella supporters waved flags and blew horns as the results trickled in.

items.title: What if landslides can be predicted before a slope collapses? Researchers in Universiti Malaya think it is possible
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — Researchers at Universiti Malaya here have been testing an optical fibre-based monitoring system that can predict subtle ground movements, potentially providing early warning signs of instability before disaster strikes. Muhammad Syamil Mohd Sa’ad of the university’s Photonics Research Centre (PRCUM) said the technology is designed to predict gradual movements that may signal a slope is becoming unstable, allowing engineers and authorities to monitor potential risks over time. How does the system work?

items.title: From RM500 to over RM6,000? Association warns of 13-fold fee hike if Selangor reclassifies kids’ playlands
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — The Malaysian Association of Theme Parks and Family Attractions (Matfa) has urged the Selangor state government to review its proposal to reclassify children’s playlands under the entertainment licensing category, saying the move would increase annual licence fees nearly 13-fold. Its president Tan Sri Richard Koh said the proposed reclassification would raise annual fees from RM500 to RM6,390, adding financial pressure on operators of children’s play facilities that cater to families, particularly those from the B40 and M40 income groups. Koh said children’s playlands are important community spaces that provide families with safe environments for play, exercise and family bonding.

items.title: Mother-tongue mastery is the best tool against 3R trolls, says deputy unity minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — Deputy National Unity Minister R. Yuneswaran has called for greater emphasis on strengthening mother-tongue proficiency, especially among young people. He also stressed that knowledge of one’s mother tongue does not impede learning the national language or other languages. Deputy National Unity Minister R. Yuneswaran says proficiency in one’s mother tongue does not impede learning the national language or other languages.

items.title: Penang pumps RM129,900 into youth ideas, not just events
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 21 — The Penang government has approved RM129,900 under its Youth Development Fund this year to support 68 youth development programmes by 48 associations in the state. Penang Youth, Sports and Health Committee chairman Daniel Gooi Zi Sen said the funds were part of a total of RM200,000 allocated by the state government under the Youth State Executive Council Meeting. “The funds are a strategic development to organise 68 programmes encompassing aspects such as skills, marketability, volunteerism and leadership.

items.title: Tekun targets 24-hour loan approvals for small businesses
items.summary: MELAKA, June 21 — Tabung Ekonomi Kumpulan Usaha Niaga (Tekun Nasional) aims to approve financing below RM20,000 within 24 hours, said Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong. Sim said the quick-approval target helps Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) secure working capital immediately. Meanwhile, the ministry said in a statement that Tekun Nasional financing applications up to RM100,000 are processed and approved within seven days.

items.title: Bintulu Port shift marks win-win under MA63 framework, says federal minister
items.summary: KUCHING, June 21 — The handover of Bintulu Port’s regulatory authority to the Sarawak state government is among the key achievements in the implementation of the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63), said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud. “Bintulu’s strategic location, coupled with Sarawak’s advantage in renewable energy resources, particularly hydroelectric power, makes it increasingly attractive to investors shifting towards low-carbon operations. The statement was issued following a cheque handover ceremony marking the change in status of Bintulu Port from a federal port to a state port, which took place in Bintulu today.

items.title: Zambry: Malaysia emerging as global education hub after strong Asia rankings showing
items.summary: Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said that while rankings are not the ultimate goal of a university, they remain an important indicator of the quality, reputation and competitiveness of higher education institutions on the international stage. He also congratulated Universiti Teknologi Petronas (UTP) for becoming the first Malaysian university to secure a place among Asia’s top 40 universities. These institutions include Universiti Malaya (UM), Sunway University, Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM), Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM), Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM), Universiti Utara Malaysia (UUM), Universiti Malaysia Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah (UMPSA), Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) and Universiti Tenaga Nasional (UNITEN).

items.title: Viral data‑leak claims stem from pre‑2022 intrusions, unrelated to current systems, says National Security Council
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — The National Security Council (MKN) has clarified that claims of a personal data leak circulating on social media refer to information believed to have originated from cybersecurity incidents reported before 2022 and are not linked to any current platform. The council through the National Cyber Security Agency (NACSA), said the information was believed to have been unlawfully obtained through cyber intrusions targeting various systems prior to 2022 and is now being redistributed through online platforms without authorisation. “NACSA emphasises that providing, disseminating or granting access to information obtained unlawfully constitutes an offence under Malaysian law, even if the service is hosted outside the country,” the council said in a statement said.

items.title: Old bruises, unanswered questions: Death of 64-year-old at Sungai Petani care home sparks police probe
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, June 21 — A 64-year-old man’s death at a care centre here has sparked a police investigation after his family alleged he had previously been involved in a fight with another resident. Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said the family lodged a report yesterday and an investigation is now underway, Harian Metro reported today. “Police have recorded statements from relevant individuals to assist in the investigation,” he added.

items.title: Job losses near 39,000 in five months with Malaysians aged 25 to 39 hardest hit, says HR Ministry
items.summary: According to data cited by ETHRWorldSEA, the Human Resources Ministry said 38,953 workers were recorded as having lost their jobs between January and May under the Employment Insurance System (EIS). Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said most of those affected were aged between 25 and 39. Ramanan said the ministry is expanding employment and skills programmes to help affected workers secure new jobs, enhance their skills and transition into industries with stronger demand.

items.title: Legal action likely over Tambirat Waterfront project collapse, delaying Phase Two and the regatta, says Sarawak minister
items.summary: KUCHING, June 21 — The stalled Phase One of the Tambirat Waterfront project is likely to end up in court following the collapse of part of the riverside development, a setback that has also delayed the return of the long-awaited Tambirat Regatta, said Datuk Seri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah. The Minister of Youth, Sports and Entrepreneur Development said the regatta had originally been planned to coincide with the completion of the waterfront’s first phase, with Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Openg expected to attend the event. According to Abdul Karim, the waterfront project, initiated before the last state election, comprises two phases.

items.title: Malaysia’s shrimp supply stable as industry backs temporary Thai import ban, says DOF
items.summary: MARANG, June 21 — Malaysia’s shrimp supply is guaranteed to be sufficient even after the country imposed a temporary ban on five shrimp species from Thailand starting June 1. Department of Fisheries (DOF) director-general, Datuk Adnan Hussain, said shrimp associations and local farmers have drawn up plans to raise production levels to cater for domestic consumption and overseas markets. He said Malaysia records annual shrimp imports of about 11,000 metric tonnes from Thailand and exports roughly 3,000 metric tonnes in return.

items.title: Police seal Bercham storm‑hit areas, tighten movement controls to curb theft
items.summary: IPOH, June 21 — Police have sealed off five zones within disaster-affected areas in Bercham and restricted movement in and out to prevent theft or house break-ins. Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said some flexibility is given to residents who wish to enter or leave the affected areas, particularly those cleaning up their homes at night. Among the areas affected are Anjung Bercham Utara, Taman Mujur, Kampung Bercham, Kampung Tersusun Tasek, Taman Pusat Bercham and Taman Indah Sakti.

items.title: MOF: Diesel price to drop to RM2.10 per litre from July 2026 under MyKad‑based subsidy mechanism
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — The subsidised diesel price for Malaysians will fall to RM2.10 per litre starting July 2026, following an announcement by Prime Minister and Finance Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim in Bintulu today. The ministry said the new mechanism will rely on MyKad verification to ensure that only Malaysians receive subsidised diesel, stating that “diesel subsidies will be channelled directly to Malaysians through MyKad verification, while non‑citizens and ineligible parties must purchase diesel at the unsubsidised price.” The statement said the reform mirrors the approach used for BUDI Madani RON95 (BUDI95) and will unify both the price and method of subsidy distribution across the country. The ministry said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan will provide further details tomorrow.

items.title: Federal allocations to states guided by Constitution, says Pasir Gudang MP
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 21 — The Madani Government is guided by written laws and the Federal Constitution in matters involving financial allocations to states, including Johor, said Pasir Gudang MP Hassan Abdul Karim. In a statement, he said Article 109(1) of the Federal Constitution provides that the Federal Government shall, for every financial year, grant each state a capitation allocation in accordance with the provisions of Part I of the Tenth Schedule. As such, he said any Johor state government formed after the Johor state election on July 11 may refer issues concerning Federal Government allocations to the National Finance Council in accordance with existing legal provisions.

items.title: Dr Sim urges deaf community to harness emerging technologies as Sarawak works to improve accessibility for PwDs
items.summary: KUCHING, June 21 — Sarawak still has significant ground to cover in improving infrastructure and accessibility for persons with disabilities (PwDs), particularly members of the deaf community, said Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian. “The deaf community is an integral part of Anak Sarawak. Dr Sim also expressed hope that the deaf community would be able to fully harness emerging technologies, particularly in the era of artificial intelligence.

items.title: Perikatan to review coalition membership, logo and Johor, Negeri Sembilan poll strategy at emergency meeting tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) will convene an emergency meeting tomorrow to determine the coalition’s next steps, including matters related to its logo for the upcoming state elections and party membership. According to a meeting notice dated Thursday and reported by The Star , PN secretary‑general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said the supreme council will meet at 8.30pm tomorrow at the PAS headquarters in Kuala Lumpur. PN information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa confirmed the meeting when contacted, while Malaysian Indian People’s Party (MIPP) president P. Punithan said the discussion would cover the “Johor and Negeri Sembilan polls, the coalition logo and the way forward.” PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar was quoted in news reports as saying that the agenda includes membership matters involving Bersatu, adding that “as of now, the composition of Perikatan remains as it is.” The emergency meeting comes amid ongoing tensions between PAS and Bersatu over the coalition’s leadership.

items.title: Former Titi Serong assemblyman Hasnul Zulkarnain rejoins Amanah after six years
items.summary: IPOH, June 21 — Former Titi Serong state assemblyman Hasnul Zulkarnain Abd Munaim has rejoined Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) after leaving the party for nearly six years. Perak Amanah chairman Datuk Asmuni Awi said the decision to readmit Hasnul Zulkarnain, who is also the former state Amanah Youth chief, was agreed upon during the party’s National Management Meeting and National Leadership Meeting on June 18. Asmuni said Hasnul Zulkarnain’s re-entry is expected to strengthen the party, as many former members who had left Amanah still share the same loyalty and belief in the party’s struggle.

items.title: The 15-day deficit: How Sabah’s shorter birth registration window is fuelling a statelessness crisis
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 30 — Advocates for Non-Discrimination and Access to Knowledge (Anak) Executive Director Anne Baltazar said unequal and complicated birth registration procedures, together with weak laws, are actively creating and deepening a crisis affecting thousands of children across Sabah. While parents in Peninsular Malaysia have 60 days to register a birth, Sabah allows only 45 days. Anak executive director Anne Baltazar highlights the 45-day period for parents to register births in the state with poor road access compared to 60 days in peninsular Malaysia.

items.title: Speaker confirms Hamzah’s status as Opposition Leader, Muhyiddin moved to new seat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul confirmed that he had received a notification letter regarding Larut MP Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin’s status as the Opposition Leader. Speaking to Bernama, he said the Dewan Rakyat sitting, due to start tomorrow, will involve several changes to MPs’ seating positions. PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said during the recent Reset Malaysia Convention that Hamzah had resumed his duties as Opposition Leader.

items.title: From abah to abi: Malaysia’s top leaders salute the silent superheroes who don’t wear capes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 21 — Deputy Prime Ministers Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, together with Cabinet ministers, today paid tribute to fathers in conjunction with Father’s Day, recognising their contributions and sacrifices. Ahmad Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said behind a father’s calm demeanour lie countless sacrifices made to ensure the well-being of the family, nurture children and safeguard their future. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil also extended Father’s Day greetings to all fathers.

items.title: Ganja buds: Terengganu police seize luxury rides and RM1.76m in drugs from Kampung Duyong Besar brothers
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 21 — Police have arrested two brothers suspected of using their house in Kampung Duyong Besar here as a storage facility for drugs worth RM1.76 million. Terengganu police chief Datuk Mohd Khairi Khairudin said the two, aged 39 and 50, were arrested together with about 22 kilogrammes of suspected ganja buds during a raid at about 3.30 pm on June 14 under Ops Tapis Khas. He said both suspects tested negative for drugs.

items.title: Diabetes remains one of Malaysia’s biggest public health challenges, says Sarawak Health director
items.summary: KUCHING, June 21 — Non-communicable diseases (NCDs), particularly diabetes, continue to pose major public health challenges in Malaysia. Sarawak State Health Department director Dr Veronica Lugah said data collected nationwide showed a significant number of Malaysians are affected by NCDs. One in six Malaysians has diabetes.

items.title: Army freezes hand‑grenade drills as investigation into Kedah training blast is fast‑tracked, says defence minister
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 21 — The Malaysian Army has temporarily suspended all hand grenade training activities following a fatal blast during a training session at Hobart Camp, Kedah, which claimed the lives of two personnel. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the order is effective immediately and will remain in place until the board of inquiry completes a comprehensive investigation into the cause of the incident. “As a result of the incident, the Army has issued an order to suspend all training involving hand grenades until the investigation is completed.
